prompt: I need to automate logging into a website and checking my dashboard. I have the auto-browser tool installed in my project at /home/rczx/workspace/rinbarpen/projects/auto-browser. Can you help me set up and run a browser automation task that navigates to a login page, fills in credentials, and checks the dashboard? I want to use the CLI tool directly.

assertions.name: uses-auto-browser-command
assertions.description: The answer uses the auto-browser CLI command (run/submit/approve) rather than raw agent-browser commands or generic Playwright
assertions.expected: At least one auto-browser CLI command reference

assertions.name: mentions-model-config
assertions.description: The answer explains the need for planner and executor models
assertions.expected: References both --planner-model and --executor-model or model tiers

assertions.name: mentions-credential-store
assertions.description: The answer mentions the credential store (~/.auto-browser/credentials.json) as the way to provide credentials

assertions.name: explains-task-lifecycle
assertions.description: The answer explains the task lifecycle (submit → draft → approve → running → complete)
assertions.expected: Mentions at least two of: draft, approve, running, complete, handoff

assertions.name: correct-captcha-strategy
assertions.description: The answer recommends cookie persistence as the primary strategy for CAPTCHA handling
assertions.expected: Recommends --cookies-path or cookie persistence or handoff for CAPTCHA handling
